blockerNotes: #2113 repository settings cleanup. #2115 blocks persistent auth-file changes. Draft #2251 remains merge-blocked by user-driven bank confirmations, synthetic bank references, cross-tenant visibility and unresolved conflicts.
currentDiscovery: The initial PostgreSQL migration creates permissive legacy deals/audit/ledger policies. VP-3.46 explicitly removes and integration-tests them before canonical policies are accepted.

vp345PhysicalRlsPolicyAlignment.layer: VP-3.45 Physical Table RLS Policy Alignment and Rehearsal

vp346RlsIntegrationHarness.layer: VP-3.46 Ephemeral PostgreSQL RLS Integration Harness
invariants: existing required API Tests workflow owns the merge-blocking RLS job
CI uses an isolated PostgreSQL 16 service with ephemeral credentials
legacy permissive migration policies are removed
canonical RLS SQL is applied only to the ephemeral database
cross-tenant deal, audit, outbox and runtime reads are denied
transaction-local context does not leak on the same database session
all ephemeral data and credentials disappear with the CI job

vp347LocalDatabaseBootstrapOrder.layer: VP-3.47 Local Database Bootstrap Order and RLS Activation

readiness: 85% honest architectural readiness. VP-3.46 validates canonical RLS behavior against an ephemeral PostgreSQL 16 database and removes legacy permissive policies. This is not production application evidence.
